steps: The vertex of a parabola lies at the midpoint of the roots: \(x_{\text{vertex}}=\frac{-3+(-1)}{2}=-2\).
Check each choice: \(x=-2\) must lie strictly inside the interval. Only \(-3<x<1\) (choice B) contains \(-2\).
choiceElimination:
• Choice A — 'negative 4 is less than x, which is less than negative 3': \(-2\) is not in \((-4,-3)\). ✗
  • Choice B — 'negative 3 is less than x, which is less than 1': \(-3 < -2 < 1\), so \(-2\) lies in this interval. ✓
  • Choice C — 'negative 2 is less than x, which is less than 3': This interval starts just above \(-2\), so \(-2\) is not contained (the left endpoint is excluded). ✗
  • Choice D — 'negative 2 is less than x, which is less than 4': Same issue — left bound is \(-2\) exclusive, so \(-2\) is not inside. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
